new_classes.label_en: still wine-like beverage from concentrated grape must in containers ≤ 2 L (CN 2206 00 51)
new_classes.label_de: Stilles Getränk aus konzentriertem Traubenmost in Behältnissen ≤ 2 L (KN 2206 00 51)
new_classes.definition_en: A still fermented beverage of heading 2206 that is not produced by the natural fermentation of fresh grape must but is instead produced from concentrated grape must, presented in containers holding not more than 2 litres. Fermentation is typically initiated with yeast and sugar may be added.

A fermentation process in which concentrated (stable, storable) grape must rather than fresh grape must is used as the substrate, typically initiated by yeast, to produce a wine-like beverage classified under heading 2206.

coverage_explanation: The axioms capture the three main distinguishing criteria for CN 2206 00 51: (1) the beverage is produced from concentrated grape must (not fresh grape must — modelled via ConcentratedGrapeMustFermentationProcess), (2) it is still (not sparkling — modelled via complement of SparklingFermentedBeverage2206), and (3) it is in containers ≤ 2 L (modelled via SmallContainer). The note also mentions yeast-initiated fermentation and the possible addition of sugar as ingredients; these are not fully axiomatised because no dedicated 'yeast ingredient' class or 'added sugar' restriction exists in the current TBox for the fermentation process, and modelling them would risk over-specification. The note reference to HS Explanatory Notes items 1–10 under heading 2206 (German note) is purely cross-referential and carries no additional product-discriminating axiom content. Coverage is therefore estimated at 0.72.
